text:
Market today: Cooler-than-expected inflation data is generally positive for growth-oriented tech and chip names. However, ongoing concerns about chip access to China could cap upside for the sector.

Your holdings: SOXL (-29.47%) and SPAL (-34.39%) are both leveraged ETFs showing significant drawdowns. These decay over time, and margin amplifies losses, so they need immediate attention. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile IPO. PTNM (-15.25%) is also a new, small spec with a notable loss.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further news regarding chip export restrictions, as this directly impacts your core holdings. Also, monitor the Fed's ongoing stance on inflation.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing your leveraged, decaying positions and respecting their stops, especially with your concentrated, margin-fueled book.
plays: SOXL: Given its -29.47% loss and leveraged decay, consider trimming or exiting if it approaches your 200 stop.
SPAL: This new, leveraged position is down -34.39%. Respect its 21.38 stop to limit further amplified losses.
No new buys today - hold and watch.
